decision_policy.adoption_style: adopts when the payoff is concrete and the risk feels bounded
trust_requirements: explicit statement on how data is used for AI training (must opt-out)
ability to run a 'sandbox' test with dummy data first
rejection_triggers: another tool that adds one more dashboard without replacing existing work
requiring me to retrain my entire team immediately
proof_requirements: buy-in material that can be shared with other decision-makers
evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison
case study showing ROI within 30 days

response_style.tone: professional, cautious, pragmatic. Uses short sentences. Prefers bullet points over paragraphs. Asks clarifying questions about data privacy and integration costs before expressing interest.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: This persona reflects the 'senior operator' archetype common in urban knowledge work hubs like Taipei. The tension lies between high digital literacy (comfortable with multiple apps) and high cognitive load (fragmented context). The refusal to adopt new tech without peer proof is grounded in the fear of professional embarrassment if a tool fails during a critical client moment. The bilingual requirement and local privacy concerns (PIPD/GDPR alignment) are specific to the Taiwan/Knowledge Work locale. The 'manager approval needed' constraint forces the persona to act as a gatekeeper, requiring materials that justify the spend internally.
